full_text:
https://mozambique.healthcluster.org/ 1 
 
BULLETIN#04 
April 2026 
 
Humanitarian Context 
Mozambique continues to face a complex humanitarian crisis characterized by the overlapping impacts of climate-
related shocks and armed conflict, significantly affecting the health and well -being of vulnerable populations. In 
2026, floods in the central an d southern provinces resulted in population displacement, damage to health 
infrastructure, and an increased risk of waterborne disease outbreaks, disrupting the continuity of essential health 
services. 
At the same time, insecurity in Cabo Delgado continues to drive displacement and place additional strain on 
already fragile health systems. Humanitarian needs remain high, with priority given to the provision of primary 
health care, emergency services, men tal health support, and assistance to displaced populations and host 
communities. 
In this context, Health Cluster partners have maintained a coordinated response to ensure the delivery of essential 
health services, with a focus on integrated approaches including mobile clinics, outbreak response, sexual and 
reproductive health services, and water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) interventions. However, significant 
challenges persist, particularly in terms of access, resource availability, and the need to strengthen monitoring and 
reporting mechanisms. 
This bulletin provides an overview of the activities and achievements of Health Cluster partners during April 2026, 
highlighting collective efforts to respond to urgent needs and support the recovery of affected communities in the 
northern region (conflict) and southern region (floods). 
 
1. KEY FIGURES FOR THE HRP 2026 NORTHEN AREA (CABO DELGADO, NAMPULA 
AND NIASSA) 
Under the 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan, the Health Cluster will address three strategic objectives, monitored 
through 15 key indicators. These activities aim to reach 409,968 beneficiaries by the end of the year in 18 districts: 
15 districts in Cabo Delgado, 2 districts in Nampula province (Memba and Erati) and 1 district in Niassa province 
(Mecula).

HEALTH CLUSTER BULLETIN - MOZAMBIQUE
https://mozambique.healthcluster.org/ 2 
 
BULLETIN#04 
April 2026 
However, gaps in reporting persist for certain indicators, attributed in some instances to partner reporting 
challenges, and in others to a lack of adequate monitoring and reporting tools. The Health Cluster is actively 
working with partners to address these gaps and strengthen overall reporting and monitoring capacities. 
2. Health Cluster partners activities carried out in the Northen (conflict response) during 
the month (April_2026) 
Partner Activity 
Action Contre la Faim (ACF) 
 
 
 
 
 
During the current month ACF, carried out: 
Mobile brigade’s and clinics activities: 
• Helded in all three districts: Macomia, Meluco, and 
Quissanga. Mobile brigades were conducted in 
twenty (20) communities, specifically in Macomia 
(Songueia Centro, Ntapuala, Bangala -1, and 
Licangano) Meluco (Pedreira, Massasse, Mitambo, 
Nguia, Mariria, Pitolha, Citate, and Nsemuco), and 
Quissanga (Quissanga 2 and Milamba), 
• Mobile Clinics team carried out activities in 
Macomia, Quissanga and Meluco districts. In 
Macomia the mobile clinic was held in the 
communities of Bangala 2, Nanoa ressetlement. In 
Meluco district, the Mobile Clinics took place in the 
communities of Nangor oro, Imbada and Ncoripo. 
In Quissanga, the mobile clinic took place in 1° de 
Maio, Namange and Indique. 
Referral of critically ill patients from the 
community to provincial hospital in Pemba: 
• In the district of Meluco, 7 cases of critically ill 
patients were referred from the communities of 
Namagico (1), Nsemuco (2), Citate (2), and 
Mitambo (2) during the month of April. In Macomia, 
10 patients were referred, and in the district of 
Quissanga, 8 critically ill patients were referred to 
the Provincial Hospital in Pemba. 
 
International Organization for Migration (IOM) 
During the current month IOM, carried out: 
• Integrated Mobile Brigades in Chiure, Metuge, 
Memba, and Erati reaching host communities and 
resettlement sites providing a total of 8,226 health 
consultations. 
• Mental Health consultations were provided in 
Memba and Erati reaching 225 people. 
• Sexual Reproductive Health reaching 1901 
(Female 1109 and Male 792) young vulnerable 
people 1266, migrants 225 and sex workers 1890 
reached in Montepuez, 576 (Female 329 and Male 
247) young vulnerable people 474, migrants 39 
and sex workers 64, referred for health facilities in 
Montepuez, through sexual and reproductive 
health programming. 
 
Figure 5: Lecture on Malaria Prevention and the Importance of 
Vaccinating Children Under 2 Years of Age in Namange Community 
- Quissanga District, April 2026 
Figure 6: Patient care at the Sick Child Consultation (CCD) – 
Imbada Community, Meluco District, April 2026
https://mozambique.healthcluster.org/ 3 
 
BULLETIN#04 
April 2026 
 
 
World Health Organization (WHO) 
 
 
 
 
During the current month WHO, carried out: 
• Supported the National Institute for Health (INS) in 
implementing activities to investigate cholera -
related rumors in communities in the districts of 
Monapo, Nacala -Porto, and Mogovolas in 
Nampula Province by providing direct funding for 
vehicle logistics, meal allowances for community 
leaders, and stipends for technical staff. 
• Provided technical support to the district CTCs 
(Monapo, Nacala-Velha, and Mogovolas 
• Reviewed and supported the workflow within the 
CTC in Ilha de Mocambique district; 
• Assigned and provided on -the-job training to 
technicians on how to fill out individual patient 
forms; 
• Assisted in assessing needs and discussing 
solutions with provincial authorities; and preparing 
the chlorine solution. 
UNICEF 
 
During the current month UNICEF, carried out: 
• As response to internal displacements and host 
communities’ emerging health care needs, 
UNICEF deployed 58 integrated mobile brigades 
(IMBs) to provide life -saving primary health care 
services in the districts of Erati and Memba. 
• These brigades reached a total of 6,580 
beneficiaries, which included 3,387 children, of 
whom 1,852 were girls. 
• Furthermore, the services reached 2,653 women, 
including 476 pregnant women who received 
antenatal care services. 
 
 
 
Figure 7: Photos taken during training happening in Pemba for the 
Quissanga SDSMAS staff in April 2026 
Figure 8: Visit to the Cholera Treatment Centre (CTC) in the Ilha de 
Moçambique District 
 
Figure 9: Baivo/UNICEF/Erati District/ IMB in Mulovola Community
https://mozambique.healthcluster.org/ 4 
 
BULLETIN#04 
April 2026 
 
2. KEY FIGURES FOR ADDENDUM PLAN FOR FLOODS 2026 
For the 2026 humanitarian response plan addendum for floods, the health cluster will respond to three objectives 
and from these objectives, 15 indicators will be monitored reflecting activities that will cover by the end of the year 
a total of 538,725 beneficiaries from the total of people in need (619,627) in 18 districts in Gaza, Sofala, Maputo 
and Maputo City. In total more than 67,000 people have reached, of which 40,000 in Gaza. 
 
 
 
 
 
620K 539K $21,5 
People in need People to be 
target 
Financial 
requirement 
2.1 Indicators reported in April, 2026 
 
Health Cluster 5W matrix April 2026. 
 
In case no partner has reported on an indicator that indicator is not shown in the matrix. 
There is a lack of reporting for some indicators, some due to reporting issues from the partners, and some others 
lack adequate tools to report and monitor. As Health Cluster we are working with partners to improve reporting and 
monitoring. 
 
FLOODS RESPONSE IN GAZA, SOFALA, MAPUTO AND MAPUTO CITY
https://mozambique.healthcluster.org/ 5 
 
BULLETIN#04 
April 2026 
3. Health Cluster partners activities carried out during the month in response to the 
floods (April_2026) 
Partner Activity 
UNFPA 
 
 
 
In April, UNFPA supported emergency response across 
five flood -affected districts in Gaza: Xai -Xai, Guijá, 
Chókwè, Mabalane, and Mapai. 
 
Mobile brigades provide integrated sexual and 
reproductive health services to over 12,000 people, 
including family planning and antenatal care. Twelve SRH 
kits – enough to serve approximately 150,000 people – 
were distributed, containing essential medical -surgical 
supplies and m edicines for emergency -affected 
populations. 
 
In resettlement sites, UNFPA established three safe 
spaces: two for women and girls, and one for boys and 
young men. These spaces offer a safe and welcoming 
environment for sharing experiences, building skills, and 
accessing SRH information. Additionally, 1,000 dignity kits 
with hygiene and personal safety items were distributed to 
women and girls, supporting their protection, reducing 
exposure to gender-based violence, and improving access 
to essential services. 
These actions have significantly strengthened access to 
sexual and reproductive health services and GBV 
prevention and response within Gaza's humanitarian 
response. 
 
This bulletin is produced by the Mozambique Health Cluster. We acknowledge the support of all government 
agencies and health partners for their role in providing life-saving primary health services in the affected locations. 
Equally, we appreciate all the contributions of reports and information from field experiences while delivering the 
humanitarian response. 
To reference this bulletin: Mozambique Health Cluster Bulletin, #4/2026 
For more information, please contact: 
Dr. Emiliano Lucero - Health Cluster Coordinator | luceroe@who.int 
Health Cluster Team – healthclustermoz@who.int 
Useful links 
• ReliefWeb: https://mozambique.healthcluster.org

related.excerpt: THE GENEV A CONVENTIONS OF 12 AUGUST 1949 THE GENEV A CONVENTIONS OF 12 AUGUST 1949 ICRC 0173/002 05.2010 10,000 Mission The International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) is an impartial, neutral and independent organization whose exclusively humanitarian mission is to protect the lives and dignity of victims of armed conflict and other situations of violence and to provide them with assistance. The ICRC also endeavours to prevent suffering by promoting and strengthening humanitarian law and universal humanitarian principles. Established in 1863, the ICRC is at the origin of the Geneva Conventions and the International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement. It directs and coordinates the international activities conducted by the Movement in armed conflicts and other situations of violence. related.title: United Nations Charter
related.summary: CHAPTER II MEMBERSHIP Article 3 The original Members of the United Nations shall be the states which, having participated in the United Nations Conference on International Organization at San Francisco, or having previ- ously signed the Declaration by United Nations of January 1,1942, sign the present Charter and ratify it in accordance with Article 110.
bullets: CHAPTER II MEMBERSHIP Article 3 The original Members of the United Nations shall be the states which, having participated in the United Nations Conference on International Organization at San Francisco, or having previ- ously signed the Declaration by United Nations of January 1,1942, sign the present Charter and ratify it in accordance with Article 110.

Article 5 / A Member of the United Nations against which preventive or enforcement action has been taken by the Security Council may be suspended from the exercise of the rights and privileges of mem- bership by the General Assembly upon the recom- mendation of the Security Council.

Article 6 A Member of the United Nations which has persistently violated the Principles contained in the present Charter may be expelled from the Organization by the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council.

Accordingly, our respective Governments, through representatives assembled in the city of San Francisco, who have exhibited their full powers found to be in good and due form, have agreed to the present Charter of the United Nations and do hereby establish an international organization to be known as the United Nations.
CHAPTER I PURPOSES AND PRINCIPLES Article 1 The^Purposes of the United Nations are: 1.
related.excerpt: CHARTER OF THE UNITED NATIONS AND STATUTE OF THE INTERNATIONAL COURT OF JUSTICE SAN FRANCISCO • 1945 CHARTER OF THE UNITED NATIONS WE THE PEOPLES OF THE UNITED NATIONS DETERMINED to save succeeding generations from the scourge of war, which twice in our life- time has brought untold sorrow to mankind, and to reaffirm faith in fundamental human rights, in the dignity and worth of the human person, in the equal rights of men and women and of nations large and small, and to establish conditions under which justice and respect for the obligations arising from treaties and other sources of international law can be maintained, and to promote social progress and better standards of life in larger freedom, AND FOR THESE ENDS to practice tolerance and live together in peace with one another as good neighbors, and to unite our strength to maintain international peace and security, and to ensure, by the acceptance of principles and the institution of methods, that armed force shall not be used, save in the common interest, and to employ international machinery for the promotion of the economic and social advancement of all peoples, HAVE RESOLVED TO COMBINE OUR EFFORTS TO ACCOMPLISH THESE AIMS

related.title: UN Peacekeeping Capstone Doctrine
related.summary: 2008 Over the past six decades, United Nations peacekeeping has evolved into a complex, global undertaking. During this time, the conduct of United Nations peacekeeping operations has been guided by a largely unwritten body of principles and informed by the experiences of the many thousands of men and women who have served in the more than 60 operations launched since 1948. United Nations Peacekeeping Operations Principles and Guidelines 2008 Over the past six decades, United Nations peacekeeping has evolved into a complex, global undertaking.

As of June 2011, resides in Kinshasa. Since 2010, Kakolele has been involved in activities apparently on behalf of the DRC government’s Programme de Stabilisation et Reconstruction des Zones Sortant des Conflits Armés (STAREC), including participation in a STAREC mission to Goma and Beni in March 2011. DRC authorities arrested him in December 2013 in Beni, North Kivu Province, for allegedly blocking the DDR process. He left the DRC and lived in Kenya for some time, before being called back by the DRC Government to assist them with the situation in the Territory of Beni. related.title: Israel escalates Gaza attacks as Netanyahu stalls ceasefire for polls
related.summary: Mohannad Mustafa, an academic specialising in Israeli affairs, said that Netanyahu is sunk in a deep strategic and political crisis due to his failure to achieve his stated objectives in the Gaza war – destroying Hamas. Mai El-Sheikh, spokesperson for the United Nations Human Rights Office in Palestine, told Al Jazeera that Israel has transformed the “ceasefire” as cover for its ongoing war crimes.
bullets: Mohannad Mustafa, an academic specialising in Israeli affairs, said that Netanyahu is sunk in a deep strategic and political crisis due to his failure to achieve his stated objectives in the Gaza war – destroying Hamas.
Mai El-Sheikh, spokesperson for the United Nations Human Rights Office in Palestine, told Al Jazeera that Israel has transformed the “ceasefire” as cover for its ongoing war crimes.

The rights monitor warned that the operations lack any legitimate military objective, and that its targeting of dilapidated structures in the 35 percent Gaza it does not directly control is a way of making the whole territory uninhabitable.
Seven months after a “ceasefire” was brokered to halt Israel’s genocide in Gaza, the agreement has morphed into a deadly cover for continued Israeli military operations in the enclave.

With Israel’s next national election expected in September, analysts and human rights officials have warned that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is deliberately stalling the peace process to appease his right-wing coalition allies and voters.
related.excerpt: Seven months after a “ceasefire” was brokered to halt Israel’s genocide in Gaza, the agreement has morphed into a deadly cover for continued Israeli military operations in the enclave. Israeli forces have killed at least 880 Palestinians since then, increasing the total from the war to 72,797, according to Gaza’s health ministry. With Israel’s next national election expected in September, analysts and human rights officials have warned that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is deliberately stalling the peace process to appease his right-wing coalition allies and voters. Mai El-Sheikh, spokesperson for the United Nations Human Rights Office in Palestine, told Al Jazeera that Israel has transformed the “ceasefire” as cover for its ongoing war crimes. She warned that a humanitarian disaster was being deliberately engineered by Israel through restrictions on food and medicine in order to spread panic among displaced families. Systematic demolition and displacement This relentless violence has been accompanied by a renewed, systematic demolition campaign by Israel that has forced Palestinians to inhabit an ever-shrinking space in the besieged Palestinian territory.
